question.text: As per SEBI (Custodian) Regulations, 1996, the registration fee payable by a custodian on being granted the certificate of registration is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On being granted the certificate of registration, the Custodian needs to pay the registration fee of Rs. 50 lakhs to SEBI. The application fee is Rs. 5 lakh, and the annual fee is Rs. 10 lakh or 0.0005% of the 'assets under custody', whichever is higher. **The Trap:** Rs. 25 lakh may seem reasonable but is not the correct amount. The annual fee structure (Rs. 10 lakh or 0.0005% of AUC) is separate from the one-time registration fee. **Cross-Exam Context:** Registration fees for custodians vs. other intermediaries are compared in NISM Series III-A exam. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The annual fee payable by reference to each financial year is due within one month of completion of the financial year.
